title: Stale doctor lookup can populate doctors for the wrong department
reasoning: The department watcher starts an async doctor lookup for every department change but does not cancel, sequence, or verify the response against the currently selected department before calling setDoctors. If a user changes from department A to B quickly and A's slower request resolves last, the form shows doctors from A while department_id is B. The submitted payload then combines the selected department with a provider_id/provider_name from a stale department list.
reproduction: Open the appointment form, select department A, immediately select department B, and have the A doctors request resolve after B. The doctors dropdown can display A doctors while department_id remains B; submitting sends mismatched department/provider data.
recommendation: Track the requested department or use an AbortController/request sequence id, and only apply setDoctors when the response still matches the latest watchedDepartment. Also clear stale doctors while loading the new department.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are linked for this feature, and the current code has no async race coverage around department changes.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock api.getDoctors so the first department request resolves after the second; assert that only the second department's doctors are rendered and submitted.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists at src/pages/AppointmentForm.jsx, and the same department watcher is still around lines 113-138. The stale-response behavior described in the finding is no longer present there: the component now declares doctorRequestId with useRef at line 80, increments it before each api.getDoctors call, returns without calling setDoctors when requestId !== doctorRequestId.current, and also increments/clears doctors when department is cleared. A related current implementation in src/pages/steps/Step3AppointmentDetails.jsx uses the same request-id guard before setDoctors for api.getDoctors responses. I could not run npm run lint because the command was rejected by the current execution policy, and package.json does not define a test script.
